feature: Alpha Decay Chart — Per-Trade Forward Return Distribution Tracker
summary: Closed three gaps in the alpha decay chart feature: (1) fixed a direction-inference bug where IBKR-backfilled trades with action=SELL and no explicit direction field were incorrectly classified as LONG — they now correctly resolve to SHORT via action→direction fallback; (2) added per-dimension segmentation — a new _dominant_dimension() helper identifies which of the 9 signal dimensions had the highest score for each trade, and get_alpha_decay_stats() now produces 9 additional dim_<name> groups (one per dimension) populated from trades that carry signal_scores; (3) extended the dashboard segment table to render dimension rows under a 'by dominant dimension' sub-header whenever at least one dimension has n > 0.
research_basis: Timothy Masters / markrbest.github.io alpha decay charts; MicroAlphas 2025 — swing-trade signal effectiveness diminishes after 3–10 days. Per-dimension segmentation reveals which specific signal types have the most persistent forward alpha.
data_notes: Existing backfilled trades have empty signal_scores so all dim_* segments start at n=0. They will populate as live bot trades accumulate — signal_scores is already stored in open_trades via signal_dispatcher.py (line 96) and persisted by log_trade.
